results.blockReason: Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 90). Defects: Some dead canvas is visible on left and bottom edges, slightly reducing canvas utilization; No imagery in STRUCTURED_PROFESSIONAL archetype is not a defect; Canvas bottom area under QR code and registration text is slightly cramped with small margins; Headline font size could be marginally larger for even stronger first glance impact

Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 88). Defects: Contact email inside a small colored pill shape is slightly less prominent than the phone number, they could be visually balanced better.; The green banners with text contain quite dense blocks, so line spacing could be slightly improved for easier reading.; The headline hierarchy could be slightly stronger with more emphasis on the key job opportunity.; The job positions box background could have slightly better contrast for easier reading.; The email contact CTA could be more prominent and visually distinct from the phone number section.; The background image is slightly busy behind the text areas, mildly impacting text legibility despite outlines.; The headline 'BILFINGER SHUTDOWN PROJECT' could be adjusted to emphasize the roles or opportunities even more distinctly.

Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 83). Defects: Top label with agency name is smaller and less visually integrated, diminishing immediate agency identity prominence.; The role headline 'BILFINGER SHUTDOWN PROJECT' could be more prominently sized or contrasted for stronger immediate candidate hook.; The lower half has unused white space, particularly below interview dates, leading to moderate dead canvas area.; The interview dates are not visually differentiated strongly enough to attract quick scanning and user attention.; The contact CTA though present, could be visually more distinct or enlarged to increase prominence.; Insufficient headline emphasis compared to agency branding at top; Noticeable dead canvas in middle area reducing visual engagement and making layout feel incomplete; Contact call-to-action is not prominent enough and slightly lost in the footer section; The job title 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' should be larger and more dominant for immediate candidate focus; Top headline partially cut off, causing legibility issues; Top section suffers from cramped vertical spacing and truncation; Heavy dead canvas below interview section, wasting canvas space
